text:
Once upon a time in a quaint village nestled between rolling hills and forested valleys, there roamed a humble man named Bartholomew. Bartholomew had an extraordinary talent—an uncanny ability to hear tiny creatures hidden in the garden. Birds chirped melodies he hadn't heard yet, bugs hummed soft chants, and the occasional butterfly would just flutter past, leaving behind a peculiar glitter.

One morning Bartholomew woke up to a symphony playing through the leaves. He squinted into the sunlight streaming through the canopy above. Instead of chirping birds, he sensed sweet, lilting notes—a cricket tapping out a love song, a moth fluttering serenades, and the occasional gentle buzz of a bee.

As he wandered through the garden, he discovered these creatures hadn’t been heard singing before. A tiny bird whose songs vibrated like tiny bells, a tiny moth with wings that shimmered with every movement, and a bee whose pollination buzz felt like soft
